description: Opinionated project layout and toolchain for dlt pipelines — filesystem discovery, static validation, scheduling metadata, checkpoints, chunked backfill, runs ledger, schema-drift reconciliation, pre-load assertions, and destination capability tiers. Wraps dlt (the ingestion primitive) with a mandatory layout and operational verbs; moves zero rows itself.
rules: dlt-ops is a convenience toolchain for dlt specifically — an opinionated project layout plus operational verbs. It moves zero rows itself (dlt owns the data write), is not an orchestrator (it feeds one), and is not built for high-load or hard-SLA systems. Never imply throughput benefits.

Adapter-gated features — runs ledger and status, checkpoints, backfill, remote clean, reconcile, and assertion quarantine — require a registered DestinationAdapter (full tier). Any destination dlt can resolve runs the core loop at core tier. Degradation is loud, never silent.

dlt-ops config keys, rule IDs, and CLI flags are a closed set defined by the toolchain; the configuration reference and `validate --show-resolved-rules` are authoritative. Do not assume a key, rule ID, or flag the docs do not list.
Failure semantics are asymmetric by contract: gates that decide what data loads fail hard; observability that only records what happened never takes a healthy run down with it.
The dlt dependency is a floor (dlt>=1.27), never a cap; users own their project's dlt version.
